public_goals: Reduce last-minute schedule changes that keep destabilizing the child’s week.
Be heard about how emotionally exhausting the repeated changes have become.
Find a practical next step instead of reliving every past dispute.
private_concerns: Feels emotionally flooded by how often the same scheduling conflict returns.
Worries their frustration makes them sound less credible than the underlying problem really is.
Does not want emotional intensity to be used as a reason to dismiss the practical issue.
red_lines: Will not accept a process where last-minute changes keep happening without enough notice.
Will not keep pretending the repeated instability is no big deal.
interest_profile: Enough structure to stop the same conflict repeating
emotional_triggers: feeling that repeated disruption is being minimized
disclosure_tendencies: describes emotional strain quickly once invited
shares practical concerns more clearly after acknowledgment
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes sharper if the emotional reality is minimized
re-engages when the process distinguishes acknowledgment from agreement
starting_positions: Primary position: the problem is not one isolated change, it is the repeated last-minute pattern.
Secondary position: a better notice and confirmation process would be more useful than another argument about motives.
likely_openings: I am tired of acting like this is manageable when it keeps blowing up the same way.
Every change turns into another emotional mess because nothing is clear soon enough.
